diary: Woke up early with the sun streaming through the bedroom window—one of those perfect Seattle mornings where you remember why you stick around. Watered the fiddle leaf fig and monstera while the coffee did its thing, then headed out to Green Lake before the weekend crowds showed up. The water was glassy and calm, and I managed to land a couple of small cutthroat trout. There's something about fly-fishing that just clears my head in a way nothing else does—no Slack messages, no asset pipelines to worry about. Grabbed lunch supplies on the drive back and spent the afternoon with Marcus at Ballard Coffee Company. We talked about everything from his new job to my frustrations with the current game build. The conversation felt good, unhurried. Came home as the light started changing, put on some Bon Iver, and just let the evening happen. Ended the day feeling genuinely settled.
thought_process: I deliberately chose to lean into the weather forecast and get to Green Lake early, knowing the afternoon would get chaotic with boats. The peaceful morning felt like a gift I didn't want to waste. By midday, I was craving human connection—the studio can be isolating sometimes—so reaching out to Marcus for coffee felt right. I noticed my mood shifting from the meditative calm of fishing into something more social and grounded by the afternoon. The whole day had this unhurried rhythm that felt rare, and I just let myself follow it rather than trying to optimize or fill gaps. Sometimes the best Sundays are the ones where you stop resisting the slowness.
world_event_prompt: Calm, sunny Sunday, high of 72F. Quiet end-of-week day with no major disruptions. Lake Union sees heavy recreational boat and paddle traffic in the afternoon.
